The following issues were found in the ER calculations
1. The value presented as final ER calculation in the ER spreadsheet is not been calculated
conservatively, considering that it has not been rounded down.
2. The calculations provided in the section E.5.1 were not included in the excel spreadsheet. Moreover,
in the section E.5.1: the quantity of days of 2016 considered in the calculations is not correct;
3. The values of CCEE were not presented in the ER calculations, which does not allow the possibility to
check the conservativeness of values used in the calculations;
4. The value of EFBM,2020 is not the latest one available at DNA’s website;
Project participant response Date : 03/09/2021
1. Calculations of BEy (= EG * EF ) were rounded down (zero digits) for conservativeness. CER
PJ,y GRID,CM,y
calculations spreadsheet was revised accordingly (CER calculation_v02.xlsx)- Please refer to sheet
“ER calculations”, range B13:I13. Emissions reductions figures throughout the MR were revised
accordingly in order to reflect this revision;
2. CER calculations spreadsheet was revised accordingly (CER calculation_v02.xlsx)in order to include
transparent calculations of the expected emissions reductions that should have been observed during
the monitoring period and its comparison to the actually obtained emissions reductions figures - Please
refer to sheet “Ex-ante versus MR”. Moreover, the quantity of days of 2016 was also corrected;
3. CER calculations spreadsheet was revised (CER calculation_v02.xlsx ) in order to depict the cross
check analysis between monitored electricity generation values and those obtained from CCEE. It is
noteworthy the fact that overall differences between monitored and CCEE values account to as little
as 0.16%. Please refer to sheet “CCEE CrossCheck”. The most conservative values between both
sources were applied in the ER calculations spreadsheet
4. The value of EF is now up to date. The data available in the spreadsheet was cross checked

Ao utilizar os certificados de energia renovável (RECs) gerados pela Testa Branca III Energia S.A.
para relatar as emissões de gases de efeito estufa do escopo 2, a Omegaapoia a redução da poluição e da
dependência de combustíveis fósseis e contribui para o aumento da participação de energias renováveis na
matriz energética global (ODS 7) através de um instrumento de redução de emissões (ODS 13).
Ao utilizar os RECspara relatar as emissões do escopo 2, a Omega(i) integra medidas da mudança do clima
nas políticas, estratégias e planejamentos nacionais e (ii) melhora a educação e aumenta a conscientização e
a capacidade humana e institucional sobre mitigação, adaptação, redução de impacto e alerta precoce da
mudança do clima.

Informações institucionais
In 2008, we embarked on a transformative journey, spearheading the revolution of the
Brazilian electricity sector. Back then, betting on 100% renewable energy generation and
sustainable practices was far from common. In a country with continental dimensions and
enormous natural resource availability, our greatest aspiration is to transform Brazil by
fostering the electrification of economic sectors still dependent on fossil fuels and
enabling free access to clean and fair-priced energy for all consumer segments.
We are a global company, a reference in clean energy investments in the Americas. Today,
we proudly stand as the largest Brazilian renewable energy generation company, capable
of supplying energy to 4.3 million homes. Our remarkable portfolio boasts an operational
installed capacity of 2,174.3 MW, with an additional 509.1 MW currently under
implementation in Brazil (Bahia) and the USA (Texas).
We believe that being sustainable is about aligning interests and generating value
together. Without people who embrace projects and identify with sustainability proposals,
we would not be able to achieve much. We are Omega because we are collectively people
who embark on a commitment to transformation. We are Omega because together, we
are more.
For more information, visit our website: https://omegaenergia.com.br/

Campos de preenchimento opcional
4.1 Informações sobre a performance da organização, em comparação com benchmarks internos (ex: outras
unidades) ou externos (ex: organizações do mesmo setor).
In 2008, we embarked on a transformative journey, spearheading the revolution of the Brazilian electricity sector.
Back then, betting on 100% renewable energy generation and sustainable practices was far from common. In a
country with continental dimensions and enormous natural resource availability, our greatest aspiration is to
transform Brazil by fostering the electrification of economic sectors still dependent on fossil fuels and enabling free
access to clean and fair-priced energy for all consumer segments.
We are a global company, a reference in clean energy investments in the Americas. Today, we proudly stand as the
largest Brazilian renewable energy generation company, capable of supplying energy to 4.3 million homes. Our
remarkable portfolio boasts an operational installed capacity of 2,174.3 MW, with an additional 509.1 MW currently
under implementation in Brazil (Bahia) and the USA (Texas). In 2022, Omega Energia produced 6,805.3 GWh, 3%
below 2021, due to natural resources below the historical average.
We believe that being sustainable is about aligning interests and generating value together. Without people who
embrace projects and identify with sustainability proposals, we would not be able to achieve much. We are Omega
because we are collectively people who embark on a commitment to transformation. We are Omega because
together, we are more.
For more information, visit our website: https://omegaenergia.com.br/
4.2 Descrição de indicadores de emissão de GEE para as atividades da organização. Por exemplo, tCO e/produtos
2
fabricados.
To calculate the company’s GHG emissions intensity, we consider all energy generated by Omega in GWh and the
emissions of Scopes 1, 2, and 3, from our operational and construction assets in Brazil, with the final result: 2.52
tCO2e / GWh of emissions intensity.
In 2022, we advanced our climate strategy and developed the Decarbonization Plan, in which our reduction targets
are calculated in intensity (GWh/tCO2e). This guarantee the possibility of monitoring our emissions according to the
company's growth projection for the coming years.
4.3 Descrição de estratégias e projetos para a gestão de emissões de GEE.
In 2022 we set significant milestones in Omega's climate journey. We obtained the gold seal in the GHG Protocol
Emissions Inventory and participated for the first time in the CDP (Carbon Disclosure Project) with a score B as a
result. Another commitment adopted in this period was adherence to the Task Force on Climate-related Financial
Disclosures (TCFD) and in 2023, the Company's Decarbonization Plan was published, prepared throughout 2022 and
which has a target of 75% reduction in the emission’s intensity of Scope 1 and 2 and set qualitative targets for the
inventory and management of scope 3 emissions, focusing on the engagement of the value chain. For the next
cycle, we intend to maintain the gold seal of our emissions inventory and evolve to score A in CDP.
4.4 Informações sobre contratos com clientes e fornecedores que incluam cláusulas vinculadas à elaboração de
inventários de GEE e/ou ao envio de informações relacionadas.
22/10/2023 03:21 11
Inventário 2022 - Omega Energia
The Decarbonization Plan, developed in collaboration with a specialized consultancy, included a strategic analysis of
the most relevant scope 3 categories for the company, considering industry benchmarking and other aspects. In this
process, categories previously reported by Omega have been reviewed, and it has been decided that the following
categories must be inventoried: Purchased Goods and Services, Waste and Effluents Generated in Operations,
Business Travel, and Commuting. In 2022, emissions from our suppliers' activities were reported under the category
"Purchased Goods and Services."
The Decarbonization Plan also established qualitative goals aimed at engaging the value chain, including the
inclusion of clauses designed to stimulate and reinforce climate commitments, with a focus on the development of
the GHG inventory of the suppliers.

inventário.
Uncertainties in estimating unavailable data: Carbon stock of our Permanent Preservation Areas and reforestation
carried out based on IPCC removal factors.

da coleta de dados, contratação de verificação externa, etc.
We have undergone independent verification, in an effort to disclose more consistent data. The inventory was
assured by KPMG in accordance with the requirements of NBR ISO 14064 standards and the Brazilian GHG Protocol
Program Specifications. We are committed to continuously improving our reporting processes, as well as the
capture and organization of information.
Currently, we are in the process of automating data collection to ensure data accuracy and optimize analysis.

Nível de Confiança
O Organismo de Verificação (OV) atribuiu o seguinte nível de confiança ao processo de verificação:
Verificação com nível de confiança razoável
“O inventário de gases de efeito estufa da organização inventariante para o ano de [ano] está materialmente
correto, é uma representação justa dos dados e informações de GEE e foi elaborado de acordo com as EPB.”
As limitações do processo de verificação foram:
Verificação com nível de confiança limitado
“Não há indícios de que o inventário de gases de efeito estufa da organização inventariante para o ano de
2022 não esteja materialmente correto, não seja uma representação justa dos dados e informações de GEE
e não tenha sido preparado de acordo com as EPB.”
As limitações do processo de verificação foram: Nosso relatório não proporciona asseguração limitada
sobre o alcance de informações futuras (como, por exemplo, metas, expectativas e ambições) e
informações descritivas que são sujeitas à avaliação subjetiva.
Inventário não verificável

SECTION A. Executive summary
KBS Certification Services Pvt. Ltd. has been commissioned by Porto do Delta Energia S.A. to perform an
independent verification of its registered CDM project, “Porto do Delta Wind Power Plant CDM Project”,
UNFCCC ref. no. 7027 for the reported GHG emission reductions for the given monitoring period 04/11/2016
to 31/12/2020 (both dates included). The CDM must undergo independent third-party verification and
certification of emission reductions as the basis for issuance of Certified Emission Reductions (CERs).
The CDM projects must undergo independent third-party verification and certification of emission reductions
as the basis for issuance of Certified Emission Reductions (CERs).
The objectives of this verification exercise are, by review of objective, to establish that:
 The project activity has been implemented and operated in accordance with the registered PDD or
any approved revised PDD;
 The actual monitoring systems and procedures are in compliance with the monitoring systems and
procedures described in the monitoring plan;
 The GHG emission reduction data and calculation have been assessed to correctly support the
emission reductions being claimed and that the reported GHG emission reduction data is free from
material misstatement;
 To verify that reported GHG emission data is sufficient supported by evidence.
Scope:
The scope of the verification is the independent and objective review and ex post determination of the
monitored reductions in GHG emission by the project activity. The verification is based on review of monitoring
report, supporting information and
(a) The registered PDD, including the monitoring plan and the corresponding validation opinion(s);
(b) Previous verification reports, deviation requests, Post registration changes;
(c) Monitoring report for the monitoring period under verification including CER calculations sheets and all
supporting documents;
(d) The applied monitoring methodology;
(e) The applied standardized baseline (if applicable);
(f) Relevant decisions, clarifications and guidance from the CMP and the CDM Executive Board;
(g) All information and references relevant to the project activity’s resulting in emission reductions
(h) The project is assessed against the requirements of the Kyoto Protocol, the CDM Modalities and
Procedures and related rules and guidance.
KBS has, based on the recommendations in the latest version of CDM Validation and Verification Standard for
project activity, employed a rule-based approach in the verification, focusing on the identification of significant
reporting risks and the reliability of project monitoring.
Description of project:
The “Porto do Delta Wind Power Plant CDM Project” consists in the construction and operation of a wind power
plant, located at Parnaíba city, Piauí state, Brazil with a generation capacity of 30.8 MW.
The plant supply clean electricity to the Brazilian National Interconnected System (SIN). The project displaces
power generation using fossil fuels and hence leads to a reduction in greenhouse gas emissions.
The project activity was implemented and the commercial operation date for the wind power plants have been
verified from the ANEEL Dispatches /21/.
Methodology:
KBS follows a rule-based verification approach, wherein, as a first step, the contract review is undertaken as
per latest version of CDM Accreditation Standard. Subsequently, after the contract is signed, the monitoring
report of the project activity is made publicly available at UNFCCC website as per CDM procedures. A desk
review of the project documentation is undertaken, which is followed by a remote site visit by the members of
verification team in accordance with the latest version of CDM AS. The verification protocol is filled by the
verification team that is based on standard auditing practices and version 03.0 of CDM VVS for project activity,
to capture the assessment of applicable CDM requirements viz., version 03.0 of CDM Project Standard for
project activity, registered PDD, applied methodology, applied standardized baseline and/or tools and recent
Version 04.0 Page 2 of 28

decisions. The verification protocol provides transparent means to record the observations and compliances
by the verification team members and the nonconformities, if any. The verification protocol is an internal
document and is available on request. Following are the major milestones for the verification under
consideration.

SECTION D. Means of verification
D.1. Desk/document review
A desk review is undertaken, involving but not limited to,
 A review of the data and information presented to verify their completeness;
 A review of the registered PDD;
 A review of the monitoring plan and monitoring methodology, paying particular attention to the
frequency of measurements, the quality of metering equipment including calibration requirements, and
the quality assurance and quality control procedures;
 An evaluation of data management and the quality assurance and quality control system in the context
of their influence on the generation and reporting of emission reductions.

As result of the COVID-19 pandemic, taking into account the rules of relevant national and local authorities
(local to the DOE offices as well as to locality of the site visits), World Health Organization (WHO)
recommendations, policies of the DOE and other relevant travel restrictions and guidance (for example, a
requirement to self-isolate upon return from specific countries), a DOE may postpone site visits for onsite
inspections required by the “Validation and Verification Standard for project activities” version 03.0 /8/.
For this project, PP has made commitment/timeline /24/. Hence, the DOE has skipped the on-site visit /25/.
However, as per the CDM EB, the DOE may use other standard auditing techniques for validation or verification
as referred to in sections 9.1.3 of the VVS-PA /8/.
Verification team has used the following alternative means for its assessment and to justify that they are
sufficient for the purpose of verification. Along with desk review, audit team has conducted remote audit
interview as follows:
 A complete desk review of the registered PDD /5/, submitted MR (initial and final versions) /1/ /2/, as

SECTION F. Internal quality control
The draft verification report prepared by team leader is reviewed by an independent technical reviewer (having
competence of relevant technical area himself/herself or through an independent technical area expert) to
confirm the internal procedures established by KBS are duly followed and the verification report/opinion is
reached in an objective manner and complies with the applicable CDM requirements.
The independent technical reviewer may approve or reject the draft verification report. The findings may be
identified even at this stage, which needs to be satisfactorily resolved, before the request for issuance is
submitted to UNFCCC. The final decision is taken by the Manager Technical and Certification. The technical
reviewer and Manager (Technical & Certification) can be same person.
The final decision is authorized by Managing Director, KBS once the report is approved by the Manager
(Technical & Certification).
SECTION G. Verification opinion
The verification team confirms that the evidence is of sufficient quantity, appropriate quality and reliable. The
reported values, notation, units and sources in the monitoring report for all the monitoring parameters have
been cross checked with the emission reduction sheet and monitoring report. During the course of verification
and remote site visit, the data submitted by PP was cross verified with the values mentioned in the emission
reduction sheet and monitoring report. The procedure for data monitoring, recording, transfer and compilation
was also verified and found in compliance with the monitoring plan as mentioned in the registered PDD.
It is confirmed by the assessment team that the reported emission reductions have been conservatively
calculated. A list of referred documents for verification is also included in Appendix 3 of this report.
Based on the information seen and evaluated we confirm that the implementation of the project activity has
resulted in 254,737 tCO e emission reductions during monitoring period 04/11/2016 to 31/12/2020 (first and
2
last days are included).

SECTION H. Certification statement
KBS Certification Services Pvt. Ltd. has been contracted by Porto do Delta Energia S.A. to undertake
independent verification and certification for the greenhouse gas (GHG) emission reductions reported from the
“Porto do Delta Wind Power Plant CDM Project” and UNFCCC Reference Number 7027 for the monitoring
period 04/11/2016 to 31/12/2020 (including both dates) in the Monitoring Report Version 1 (first version) dated
10/11/2021.
The verification is based on the approved PDD and the monitoring report for this project. Our verification
approach was based on the requirements as defined under the Kyoto Protocol, as well as those defined by
the CDM Executive Board.
The management of the Porto do Delta Energia S.A. is responsible for the preparation of the GHG emissions
data and the reported GHG emissions reductions on the basis set out within the project Final Monitoring
Report, version 2.1 dated 26/01/2022. The calculation and determination of GHG emission reductions from
the project is the responsibility of the management of the Porto do Delta Energia S.A. The development and
maintenance of records and reporting procedures are in accordance with the Monitoring Report Version 2.1
dated 26/01/2022.
It is our responsibility to express an independent GHG verification opinion on the GHG emissions and on the
calculation of GHG emission reductions from the project for the period 04/11/2016 up to 31/12/2020 (including
both dates) based on the reported emission reductions in the Final Monitoring Report Version 2.1 dated
26/01/2022 for the same period.
Based on an understanding of the risks associated with reporting GHG emissions data and the controls in
place to mitigate these, KBS planned and performed our work to obtain the information and explanations that
we considered necessary to provide sufficient evidence for us to give reasonable assurance that this reported
amount of GHG emission reductions for the period is fairly stated.

SECTION A. Executive summary
Brief summary of the project activity
The project activity consists in the installation and operation a Wind power Complex Santa Vitoria do Palmar
and Chuí. This complex aggregates sixteen (16) wind power plants, totalizing 402 MW of installed capacity.
The wind power plants are distributed as follows:
Wind power plant Location Aerorenerator Turbines Installed capacity
(municipality) Model / Capacity quantity (MW)

TOTAL - - 201 402
All wind farms are located in Chuí and Santa Vitória do Palmar Municiapalities, State of Rio Grande do Sul,
Brazil, as detailed in table above.
The plants are connected to the Brazilian National Interconnected System (SIN) by Santa Vitória do Palmar
Substation.
The project activity reduces the GHG emissions through dispatching GHG-free electricity to the Brazilian
National Interconnected System.
The operation start date of the 1st wind power plants (Verace I to Verace X) included in the project activity was
on 24/02/201517/ (Liberation of operation by ANEEL).
Scope of verification
WayCarbon Soluções Ambientais e Projetos de Carbono Ltda. has contracted Earthood Services Private
Limited to conduct the verification and certification of emission reductions reported for the CDM project activity
“Grid connected electricity generation from renewable source: Windfarm Complex Santa Vitória do Palmar and
Chuí”, for the period from 16/04/2016 to 31/12/2020 (including both days).
The verification is the periodic independent review and ex post determination of the monitored reductions in
GHG emissions that have occurred due to the registered CDM project activity during the defined monitoring
period.
The scope of the verification is to establish/verify that:
Version 04.0 Page 3 of 33
CDM-VCR-FORM
• the project activity has been implemented and operated as per the registered PDD or any approved
revised PDD, and that all physical features (technology, project equipment, and monitoring and
metering equipment) of the project are in place;
• the monitoring report and other supporting documents provided are complete in accordance with the
latest applicable version of the completeness checklist for requests for issuance of CERs, verifiable,
and in accordance with applicable CDM requirements;
• the actual monitoring systems and procedures comply with the monitoring systems and procedures
described in the monitoring plan, any revised approved monitoring plan, the approved methodology
including applicable tool(s) and/or, where applicable, the approved standardized baseline;
• the data recorded and stored as per the monitoring methodology including applicable tool(s) and,
where applicable, the standardized baseline.
Verification process
The verification process involved following:
• Contract with WayCarbon for the scope of verification;
• publication of monitoring report;
• desk review;
• on-site inspection;
• issuance of verification findings;
• reporting, calculation checks, QA/QC and resolution of findings;
• issuance of draft verification report;
• independent technical review of the project documentation;
• issuance of the final verification report;
• submission of the request for issuance, as appropriate.
Conclusion
Earthood Services Private Limited has performed the verification of the CDM PA Grid connected electricity
generation from renewable source: Windfarm Complex Santa Vitória do Palmar and Chuí”, having UNFCCC
Ref. Number 8012 for the monitoring period from 16/04/2016 to 31/12/2020”. The verification team has
confirmed the implementation of the project as per description in the PDD, the monitoring plan of the PDD and
the application of the monitoring methodology (ACM0002. ver. 12.2.0). In addition, it was confirmed that the
monitoring system is in place and the emission reductions are calculated without material misstatements.
The verified emission reductions amount to 2,477,517 tCO e in the above mentioned monitoring period.
2
The verification team concluded that the registered CDM PA complies with all relevant CDM
procedures/standards/guidance and therefore request for issuance is being submitted in accordance with the
CDM procedures.

SECTION D. Means of verification
D.1. Desk/document review
A desk review was conducted by the verification team that included:
a. a review of the data and information presented to verify its completeness;
b. a review of the registered monitoring plan, the monitoring methodology including applicable
tool(s) and, where applicable, the applied standardized baseline, paying particular attention
to the frequency of measurements, the quality of metering equipment including calibration
requirements, and the quality assurance and quality control procedures;
c. an evaluation of data management and the quality assurance and quality control system in
the context of their influence on the generation and reporting of emission reductions.
A complete list of documents/evidences reviewed is included as Appendix 3.

As issues were observed in the ER calculations, a CAR has been raised..
Findings CAR 01
The following issues were found in the ER calculations
1. The value presented as final ER calculation in the ER spreadsheet is not been
calculated conservatively, considering that it has not been rounded down.
2. The calculations provided in the section E.5.1 were not included in the excel
spreadsheet. Moreover, in the section E.5.1: the quantity of days of 2016
considered in the calculations Is not correct;
3. The values of CCEE were not presented in the ER calculations, which does
not allow the possibility to check the conservativeness of values used in the
calculations;
4. The value of EFB M,2020 is not the latest one available at DNA’s website;
Conclusion The verification team confirms that:
a. the monitored data was available in accordance with the registered
monitoring plan for the operational period of the wind farm complex;
b. the reported data were crosschecked, as prescribed in the revised
approved PDD, with the relevant supporting and were found consistent;
c. appropriate methods and formulae for calculating baseline GHG
emissions have been followed;
d. the assumptions, emission factors and default values that were applied in
the calculations are correct and evidenced;
e. the calculations are transparent, consistent, correct and complete.

SECTION F. Internal quality control
The draft verification report that is prepared by verification team is reviewed by an independent technical review
team (one or more members) to confirm if the internal procedures established and implemented by ESPL were
duly complied with and such opinion/conclusion is reached in an objective manner that complies with the
applicable CDM rules/requirements.
The technical review team is collectively required to possess the technical expertise of all the technical
area/sectoral scope to which the project activity is related. All members of technical review team are
independent of the verification team.
During the technical review process, additional findings may be identified or the closed out findings may be
opened, which needs to be satisfactorily resolved before the request for issuance is submitted to UNFCCC.
The independent technical reviewer may either approve the report as such or reject/return the same in such
case providing the comments/findings/issues that needs to be resolved by the verification team. The decision
taken by the technical reviewer is final and is authorized on behalf of ESPL

reasonable assurance that reported GHG emission reductions are fairly stated.

The following issues were found in the ER calculations
1. The value presented as final ER calculation in the ER spreadsheet is not been calculated
conservatively, considering that it has not been rounded down.
2. The calculations provided in the section E.5.1 were not included in the excel spreadsheet. Moreover,
in the section E.5.1: the quantity of days of 2016 considered in the calculations is not correct;
3. The values of CCEE were not presented in the ER calculations, which does not allow the possibility to
check the conservativeness of values used in the calculations;
4. The value of EFBM,2020 is not the latest one available at DNA’s website;
Project participant response Date : 03/09/2021
1. Calculations of BEy (= EG * EF ) were rounded down (zero digits) for conservativeness. CER
PJ,y GRID,CM,y
calculations spreadsheet was revised accordingly (CER calculation_v02.xlsx)- Please refer to sheet
“ER calculations”, range B13:I13. Emissions reductions figures throughout the MR were revised
accordingly in order to reflect this revision;
2. CER calculations spreadsheet was revised accordingly (CER calculation_v02.xlsx)in order to include
transparent calculations of the expected emissions reductions that should have been observed during
the monitoring period and its comparison to the actually obtained emissions reductions figures - Please
refer to sheet “Ex-ante versus MR”. Moreover, the quantity of days of 2016 was also corrected;
3. CER calculations spreadsheet was revised (CER calculation_v02.xlsx ) in order to depict the cross
check analysis between monitored electricity generation values and those obtained from CCEE. It is
noteworthy the fact that overall differences between monitored and CCEE values account to as little
as 0.16%. Please refer to sheet “CCEE CrossCheck”. The most conservative values between both
sources were applied in the ER calculations spreadsheet
4. The value of EF is now up to date. The data available in the spreadsheet was cross checked
BM, 2020
with the latest version available at DNA`s website
(https://antigo.mctic.gov.br/mctic/opencms/ciencia/SEPED/clima/textogeral/emissao_despacho.html)
. Access on: September 03.
Documentation provided by project participant
CER calculation_v02.xlsx
Monitoring Report.v05
DOE assessment Date: 11/09/2021
1. The results of the ER calculations are now conservative
2. The estimated calculations ex-ante calculated for corresponding monitoring period are now duly
demonstrated and traceable in the ER calculations spreadsheet
3. The cross-check between monitored data and provided by public source (CCEE) have been duly
compared and the most conservative values have been applied in the ER calculations spreadsheet
4. The EFBM 2020 value has been updated as per data provided by Brazilian DNA.
CAR is closed

reported against consumption in the country during the reporting year stated. I-RECs are assigned to a beneficiary at
redemption and cannot be further assigned to a third party. No other use of these I-RECs is valid under the I-REC
Standard.
Where offset attributes are 'inc' the device registrant, who exclusively holds the environmental attribute rights, has
undertaken never to release carbon offsets in association with these MWh; 'excl' means carbon offsets relating to these
MWh may be traded independently at some point in the future.
For labelling scheme information please refer to the scheme's website. Labelling scheme listing may not be exhaustive.
Thermal plant emit carbon as part of the combustion process. Whilst this is not zero carbon, it is generally recognised
as carbon neutral where the source is recent biomass.

Serena Energia S.A

Aline Macedo (aline.macedo@srna.co)
Informações institucionais:
On the back of a 15-year legacy that fills us with pride, in November 2023 we
announced a new stage in our journey, involving a new identity and new market
positioning with the launch of our auspicious new brand: Serena. To achieve our
goal, the new branding is in line with the new era of energy freedom. Our aim is to
increase our activities with small and medium-sized companies (PMEs), and then
with residential consumers looking for energy residential consumers that is cleaner
and more accessible, due to society’s desire for a more sustainable world. This is a
timeinwhicheveryonecancollaboratetoprosperthrough100%renewableenergy.
Serena as a brand is also related to this new moment of internationalization of the
Company, in which we use a single brand in both Brazil and the United States. We
began operations in the United States with completion of the construction phase of
the Goodnight 1 project, this being our first step towards meeting the needs of
consumers in Texas, the second biggest free energy market in the United States.
Now, we have 2,683.3 MW of operational installed capacity and 6,541 MW under
construction and in the development pipeline. In 2023, we produced clean energy
capableofpowering4.4millionhomes.
The new identity encompasses and presents our essence and vision, reflecting the
strong spirit of community and the creation of a network of prosperity aligned with
theconsolidationofgoodoperationalresultsandbusinessexpansion.Changeisan
opportunitytodobetterandgeneratemoreprosperityforall.
Learnmoreathttps://srna.co/.

Serena has operations in seven Brazilian states and in the United States, offering sustainable energy through the
development and operation of wind and solar projects for different types of consumers and companies, involving
innovative technological solutions to expand access and reduce the cost of clean energy for our clients. Our
involvementrangesfromdevelopmentandimplementationthroughtotheoperationandgenerationofwindand
solar energy, which also includes the operation of Small Hydroelectric Power Stations (HPS). We ended 2023 with
2,772.8 MW in our portfolio, comprising 88% wind, 9% solar, and 3% hydro. Additionally, we have 6,451.3 MW in
thedevelopmentpipeline.In2023,wegenerated8,668.4GWhofcleanenergy,27%morethanin2022.
Consideringfinancialindicators,weended2023witharecordEBITDAofR$1.64billion,equalingannualgrowthof
39%andsurpassingourguidancefortheyearby9%.Thisresultrepresentsa17xincreasesinceourIPOin2017.
Finally,regardingGHGemissions,wereducedtotalcarbonemissionsinourBraziloperationsby62%comparedto
thebaseyearof2021withtheimplementationoftheDecarbonizationPlanapprovedin2023.
Learnmoreaboutoperationalandfinancialdataonourinvestorrelationswebsite:https://ri.srna.co/en/.
4.2DescriçãodeindicadoresdeemissãodeGEEparaasatividadesdaorganização.Porexemplo,tCO2e/produtos
fabricados.
To determine the intensity of the company’s greenhouse gas emissions, we considered the sum of Scopes 1, 2,
and 3, following GRI 305-4, relative to all energy produced by Serena Energia at its operational units located in
BrazilandtheUnitedStates.Thefinalresultwas1tCO2e/GWh.
Nonetheless, the company's Decarbonization Plan focuses on the emissions intensity of Scope 1 + 2 in Brazil as
the measurement’s unit for its reduction target to ensure that our emissions can be monitored in line with the
company's growth projections for the coming years. For the Brazil operation, which is the target of the
DecarbonizationPlan,theemissionsintensity2023amountedto0.37tCO2e/GWh.
4.3DescriçãodeestratégiaseprojetosparaagestãodeemissõesdeGEE.
In terms of GHG emissions management, we completed 100% of the actions set in the Decarbonization Plan for
2023. In Scope 1 emissions, we optimized stationary combustion equipment testing at the Assuruá unit. In
addition, we made progress on two actions planned for the following years: replacing the use of fossil fuels with
biofuels,reachingapercentageof40%inthefirstyearofreplacinggasolinewithethanol,aswellasstandardizing
the Maintenance and Operation Plan for air conditioning equipment at two operating units. For Scope 2
emissions, in August 2023, Serena signed an energy sales agreement with the corporate office headquarters
building,andnowalltheenergyconsumedcomesfroma100%renewableandcertifiedsource.Thoseactionsled
toanapproximate80%reductionindirectGHGemissions(Scopes1and2)forourBraziloperationscomparedto
the2021baselineemissions.
Regarding indirect emissions management (Scope 3), in 2023, we implemented an automated waste data
management system and conducted emissions inventory training for our suppliers, achieving a 62% participation
rate.

As emissões de gases de efeito estufa (GEE) informadas pela Organização Inventariante em seu inventário de
emissões, de 1º de janeiro até 31 de dezembro de 2023, são verificáveis e cumprem os requisitos do
Programa Brasileiro GHG Protocol, detalhados nas Especificações do Programa Brasileiro GHG Protocol de
Contabilização, Quantificação e Publicação de Inventários Corporativos de Emissões de Gases de Efeito Estufa
(EPB).
Nível de Confiança
O Organismo de Verificação (OV) atribuiu o seguinte nível de confiança ao processo de verificação:
Verificação com nível de confiança razoável
“O inventário de gases de efeito estufa da organização inventariante para o ano de [ano] está materialmente
correto, é uma representação justa dos dados e informações de GEE e foi elaborado de acordo com as EPB.”
As limitações do processo de verificação foram:
Verificação com nível de confiança limitado
“Não há indícios de que o inventário de gases de efeito estufa da organização inventariante para o ano de
2023 não esteja materialmente correto, não seja uma representação justa dos dados e informações de GEE e
não tenha sido preparado de acordo com as EPB.”
As limitações do processo de verificação foram:
Nosso relatório não proporciona asseguração limitada sobre o alcance de informações futuras (como, por
exemplo, metas, expectativas e ambições) e informações descritivas que são sujeitas à avaliação subjetiva.
Inventário não verificável
Incluir razão, por exemplo: “devido a erros de dados” ou “não está de acordo com as EPB”:

SECTION A. Executive summary
KBS Certification Services Pvt. Ltd. has been commissioned by Porto do Delta Energia S.A. to perform an
independent verification of its registered CDM project, “Porto do Delta Wind Power Plant CDM Project”,
UNFCCC ref. no. 7027 for the reported GHG emission reductions for the given monitoring period 04/11/2016
to 31/12/2020 (both dates included). The CDM must undergo independent third-party verification and
certification of emission reductions as the basis for issuance of Certified Emission Reductions (CERs).
The CDM projects must undergo independent third-party verification and certification of emission reductions
as the basis for issuance of Certified Emission Reductions (CERs).
The objectives of this verification exercise are, by review of objective, to establish that:
 The project activity has been implemented and operated in accordance with the registered PDD or
any approved revised PDD;
 The actual monitoring systems and procedures are in compliance with the monitoring systems and
procedures described in the monitoring plan;
 The GHG emission reduction data and calculation have been assessed to correctly support the
emission reductions being claimed and that the reported GHG emission reduction data is free from
material misstatement;
 To verify that reported GHG emission data is sufficient supported by evidence.
Scope:
The scope of the verification is the independent and objective review and ex post determination of the
monitored reductions in GHG emission by the project activity. The verification is based on review of monitoring
report, supporting information and
(a) The registered PDD, including the monitoring plan and the corresponding validation opinion(s);
(b) Previous verification reports, deviation requests, Post registration changes;
(c) Monitoring report for the monitoring period under verification including CER calculations sheets and all
supporting documents;
(d) The applied monitoring methodology;
(e) The applied standardized baseline (if applicable);
(f) Relevant decisions, clarifications and guidance from the CMP and the CDM Executive Board;
(g) All information and references relevant to the project activity’s resulting in emission reductions
(h) The project is assessed against the requirements of the Kyoto Protocol, the CDM Modalities and
Procedures and related rules and guidance.
KBS has, based on the recommendations in the latest version of CDM Validation and Verification Standard for
project activity, employed a rule-based approach in the verification, focusing on the identification of significant
reporting risks and the reliability of project monitoring.
Description of project:
The “Porto do Delta Wind Power Plant CDM Project” consists in the construction and operation of a wind power
plant, located at Parnaíba city, Piauí state, Brazil with a generation capacity of 30.8 MW.
The plant supply clean electricity to the Brazilian National Interconnected System (SIN). The project displaces
power generation using fossil fuels and hence leads to a reduction in greenhouse gas emissions.
The project activity was implemented and the commercial operation date for the wind power plants have been
verified from the ANEEL Dispatches /21/.
Methodology:
KBS follows a rule-based verification approach, wherein, as a first step, the contract review is undertaken as
per latest version of CDM Accreditation Standard. Subsequently, after the contract is signed, the monitoring
report of the project activity is made publicly available at UNFCCC website as per CDM procedures. A desk
review of the project documentation is undertaken, which is followed by a remote site visit by the members of
verification team in accordance with the latest version of CDM AS. The verification protocol is filled by the
verification team that is based on standard auditing practices and version 03.0 of CDM VVS for project activity,
to capture the assessment of applicable CDM requirements viz., version 03.0 of CDM Project Standard for
project activity, registered PDD, applied methodology, applied standardized baseline and/or tools and recent
Version 04.0 Page 2 of 28
CDM-VCR-FORM
decisions. The verification protocol provides transparent means to record the observations and compliances
by the verification team members and the nonconformities, if any. The verification protocol is an internal
document and is available on request. Following are the major milestones for the verification under
consideration.
Publication of MR 12/11/2021
Remote site verification 10/12/2021
Draft Verification Report 13/12/2021
Final Verification Report 04/04/2022
KBS Certification Services Pvt. Ltd. Confirms that the monitoring system is in place and the emission
reductions are calculated without material misstatements.

SECTION D. Means of verification
D.1. Desk/document review
A desk review is undertaken, involving but not limited to,
 A review of the data and information presented to verify their completeness;
 A review of the registered PDD;
 A review of the monitoring plan and monitoring methodology, paying particular attention to the
frequency of measurements, the quality of metering equipment including calibration requirements, and
the quality assurance and quality control procedures;
 An evaluation of data management and the quality assurance and quality control system in the context
of their influence on the generation and reporting of emission reductions.

SECTION F. Internal quality control
The draft verification report prepared by team leader is reviewed by an independent technical reviewer (having
competence of relevant technical area himself/herself or through an independent technical area expert) to
confirm the internal procedures established by KBS are duly followed and the verification report/opinion is
reached in an objective manner and complies with the applicable CDM requirements.
The independent technical reviewer may approve or reject the draft verification report. The findings may be
identified even at this stage, which needs to be satisfactorily resolved, before the request for issuance is
submitted to UNFCCC. The final decision is taken by the Manager Technical and Certification. The technical
reviewer and Manager (Technical & Certification) can be same person.
The final decision is authorized by Managing Director, KBS once the report is approved by the Manager
(Technical & Certification).
SECTION G. Verification opinion
The verification team confirms that the evidence is of sufficient quantity, appropriate quality and reliable. The
reported values, notation, units and sources in the monitoring report for all the monitoring parameters have
been cross checked with the emission reduction sheet and monitoring report. During the course of verification
and remote site visit, the data submitted by PP was cross verified with the values mentioned in the emission
reduction sheet and monitoring report. The procedure for data monitoring, recording, transfer and compilation
was also verified and found in compliance with the monitoring plan as mentioned in the registered PDD.
It is confirmed by the assessment team that the reported emission reductions have been conservatively
calculated. A list of referred documents for verification is also included in Appendix 3 of this report.
Based on the information seen and evaluated we confirm that the implementation of the project activity has
resulted in 254,737 tCO e emission reductions during monitoring period 04/11/2016 to 31/12/2020 (first and

independent verification and certification for the greenhouse gas (GHG) emission reductions reported from the

period 04/11/2016 to 31/12/2020 (including both dates) in the Monitoring Report Version 1 (first version) dated
10/11/2021.
The verification is based on the approved PDD and the monitoring report for this project. Our verification
approach was based on the requirements as defined under the Kyoto Protocol, as well as those defined by
the CDM Executive Board.
The management of the Porto do Delta Energia S.A. is responsible for the preparation of the GHG emissions
data and the reported GHG emissions reductions on the basis set out within the project Final Monitoring
Report, version 2.1 dated 26/01/2022. The calculation and determination of GHG emission reductions from
the project is the responsibility of the management of the Porto do Delta Energia S.A. The development and
maintenance of records and reporting procedures are in accordance with the Monitoring Report Version 2.1
dated 26/01/2022.
It is our responsibility to express an independent GHG verification opinion on the GHG emissions and on the
calculation of GHG emission reductions from the project for the period 04/11/2016 up to 31/12/2020 (including
both dates) based on the reported emission reductions in the Final Monitoring Report Version 2.1 dated
26/01/2022 for the same period.
Based on an understanding of the risks associated with reporting GHG emissions data and the controls in
place to mitigate these, KBS planned and performed our work to obtain the information and explanations that
we considered necessary to provide sufficient evidence for us to give reasonable assurance that this reported
amount of GHG emission reductions for the period is fairly stated.

SECTION A. Executive summary
KBS Certification Services Pvt. Ltd. has been commissioned by Omega Energia Renovavel S.A. to perform
an independent verification of its registered CDM project, “Delta do Parnaíba Wind Power Plant Complex CDM
Project”, UNFCCC ref. no. 8021 for the reported GHG emission reductions for the given monitoring period
01/03/2013 to 29/02/2020 (both dates included). The CDM must undergo independent third-party verification
and certification of emission reductions as the basis for issuance of Certified Emission Reductions (CERs).
The CDM projects must undergo independent third-party verification and certification of emission reductions
as the basis for issuance of Certified Emission Reductions (CERs).
The objectives of this verification exercise are, by review of objective, to establish that:
 The project activity has been implemented and operated in accordance with the registered PDD or
any approved revised PDD;
 The actual monitoring systems and procedures are in compliance with the monitoring systems and
procedures described in the monitoring plan;
 The GHG emission reduction data and calculation have been assessed to correctly support the
emission reductions being claimed and that the reported GHG emission reduction data is free from
material misstatement;
 To verify that reported GHG emission data is sufficient supported by evidence.
Scope:
The scope of the verification is the independent and objective review and ex post determination of the
monitored reductions in GHG emission by the project activity. The verification is based on review of monitoring
report, supporting information and
(a) The registered PDD or any approved revised PDD, including the monitoring plan and the corresponding
validation opinion(s);
(b) Previous verification reports, deviation requests, Post registration changes;
(c) Monitoring report for the monitoring period under verification including CER calculations sheets and all
supporting documents;
(d) The applied monitoring methodology;
(e) The applied standardized baseline (if applicable);
(f) Relevant decisions, clarifications and guidance from the CMP and the CDM Executive Board;
(g) All information and references relevant to the project activity’s resulting in emission reductions
(h) The project is assessed against the requirements of the Kyoto Protocol, the CDM Modalities and
Procedures and related rules and guidance.
KBS has, based on the recommendations in the latest version of CDM Validation and Verification Standard for
project activity, employed a rule-based approach in the verification, focusing on the identification of significant
reporting risks and the reliability of project monitoring.
Description of project:
The “Delta do Parnaíba Wind Power Plant Complex CDM Project” is a complex project composed of three
wind power plants (WPPs), Delta do Parnaíba, Porto das Barcas and Porto Salgado, located at Parnaíba city,
Piauí state, Brazil with a total generation capacity of 70 MW.
All plants supply clean electricity to the Brazilian National Interconnected System (SIN). The project displaces
power generation using fossil fuels and hence leads to a reduction in greenhouse gas emissions.
The project activity was implemented and the commercial operation date for the wind power plants have been
verified from the ANEEL Dispatches /20/.
Methodology:
KBS follows a rule-based verification approach, wherein, as a first step, the contract review is undertaken as
per latest version of CDM Accreditation Standard. Subsequently, after the contract is signed, the monitoring
report of the project activity is made publicly available at UNFCCC website as per CDM procedures. A desk
review of the project documentation is undertaken, which is followed by a remote site visit by the members of
verification team in accordance with the latest version of CDM AS. The verification protocol is filled by the
verification team that is based on standard auditing practices and version 03.0 of CDM VVS for project activity,
to capture the assessment of applicable CDM requirements viz., version 03.0 of CDM Project Standard for
Version 04.0 Page 3 of 38
CDM-VCR-FORM
project activity, registered PDD or any approved revised PDD, applied methodology, applied standardized
baseline and/or tools and recent decisions. The verification protocol provides transparent means to record the
observations and compliances by the verification team members and the nonconformities, if any. The
verification protocol is an internal document, and is available on request. Following are the major milestones
for the verification under consideration.
Publication of MR 12/11/2021
Remote site verification 09 - 10/12/2021

Final Verification Report 05/02/2022

 A review of the data and information presented to verify their completeness;
 A review of the approved revised PDD;
 A review of the monitoring plan and monitoring methodology, paying particular attention to the
frequency of measurements, the quality of metering equipment including calibration requirements, and
the quality assurance and quality control procedures;
 An evaluation of data management and the quality assurance and quality control system in the context
of their influence on the generation and reporting of emission reductions.

E.7. Compliance with the calibration frequency requirements for measuring instruments
Means of verification The calibration certificates were checked in order to verify the compliance and the
calibration frequency of each measuring equipment.
The calibration frequency of the electricity meters is 5 years from 01/01/2017
onwards as per national standard /30/ and defined in the approved revised PDD
/5/. Therefore, as per national standard /30/ from the starting date of the first
crediting period until 31/12/2016, meters calibration should happen each 2 (two)
years and from 01/01/2017, each 5 (five years).
As check in the calibration certificates /34/ /35/ /36/, the calibration of measuring
equipment was carried out by an accredited person or institution.

for project activities /8/;

SECTION F. Internal quality control
The draft verification report prepared by team leader is reviewed by an independent technical reviewer (having
competence of relevant technical area himself/herself or through an independent technical area expert) to
confirm the internal procedures established by KBS are duly followed and the verification report/opinion is
reached in an objective manner and complies with the applicable CDM requirements.
The independent technical reviewer may approve or reject the draft verification report. The findings may be
identified even at this stage, which needs to be satisfactorily resolved, before the request for issuance is
submitted to UNFCCC. The final decision is taken by the Manager Technical and Certification. The technical
reviewer and Manager (Technical & Certification) can be same person.
The final decision is authorized by Managing Director, KBS once the report is approved by the Manager
(Technical & Certification).
SECTION G. Verification opinion
The verification team confirms that the evidence is of sufficient quantity, appropriate quality and reliable. The
reported values, notation, units and sources in the monitoring report for all the monitoring parameters have
been cross checked with the emission reduction sheet and monitoring report. During the course of verification
and remote site visit, the data submitted by PP was cross verified with the values mentioned in the emission
reduction sheet and monitoring report. The procedure for data monitoring, recording, transfer and compilation
was also verified and found in compliance with the monitoring plan as mentioned in the approved revised PDD.
It is confirmed by the assessment team that the reported emission reductions have been conservatively
calculated. A list of referred documents for verification is also included in Appendix 3 of this report.
Based on the information seen and evaluated we confirm that the implementation of the project activity has
resulted in 749,473 tCO e emission reductions during monitoring period 01/03/2013 to 29/02/2020 (first and
2
last days are included).

Valid calibration certificates/reports throughout the monitoring period sent (summary in file “Medidores de
Faturamento-DP-220110.xlsx”). Description revised in monitoring report.
Please note that the monitoring period for the CDM project activity starts on 03-Jan-2013, the date on which
the plants were expected to start operation. Nevertheless, as explained in the MR, the operation only started
in July 2014 (no electricity generation dispatched to the grid before and, consequently, no CERs before).
For that reason, the first calibration took place only on 16 Sep 2013. It is also important to note that this first
calibration is the so called “manufacturer’s calibration”, that is, the purchased equipment is calibrated at the
manufacturer’s site; before being purchased, shipped, and used, the “manufacturer’s calibration” is
confirmed/validated during commissioning (carried out in 2-3 April 2014), when the two years interval for the
next regular calibration starts counting (Source: ONS, 2011. Procedimentos de Rede, Submódulo 12.3 -

The next regular calibration was due by 1 April 2016, but it was carried out in June 2016 (report approval
on 13 Jun 2016). The performed calibration showed that all meters were properly operating below the
maximum permissible error of the instrument of 0.2% (see sheet “calibration 2016” in the spreadsheet
“Medidores de Faturamento-DP-220110.xlsx”). In accordance with CDM-VVS for Proj. Act. (v. 03.0,
paragraph 366), the maximum permissible error of the equipment was conservatively applied (discounted)

Informações institucionais:
Serena is a Brazilian renewable energy company that operates across the entire
clean energy generation cycle — from the development, construction, and
management of power plants to sharing prosperity with end users. Our digital
energy comes from the best wind and solar resources in the country, harnessing
nature’s potential to deliver sustainable, high-quality power with no unnecessary
burdenontheenvironment.
With a diversified and resilient portfolio located in regions with the best climate
conditions across the Americas, Serena delivers energy with excellence, low risk,
and strong returns. In February 2025, our portfolio includes 2,801.2 MW in
operation, 2.5 MW under construction, and a development pipeline totaling 6,540.8
MW.
Sustainability is at the core of our operations. We adopt state-of-the-art clean
technologies to support the energy transition and ensure a reliable, long-term
energy supply. Serena is constantly investing to be a source of limitless clean
energy,creatingatruecycleofprosperity—fromnaturetostakeholders.

4.1 Informações sobre a performance da organização, em comparação com benchmarks internos (ex: outras
unidades)ouexternos(ex:organizaçõesdomesmosetor).
Serena has operations in seven Brazilian states and in the United States, offering sustainable energy through the
developmentandoperationofwindandsolarprojectsfordifferenttypesofconsumersandcompanies,involving
innovative technological solutions to expand access and reduce the cost of clean energy for our clients. Our
involvementrangesfromdevelopmentandimplementationthroughtotheoperationandgenerationofwindand
solar energy, which also includes the operation of Small Hydroelectric Power Stations (HPS). We ended 2024 with
2,801.2MWinourportfolio,comprising94%wind,3%solar,and3%hydro.Additionally,wehave6,540,8MWin
thedevelopmentpipeline.In2024,wegenerated10,257GWhofcleanenergy.Consideringfinancialindicators,we
ended 2024 with a record EBITDA of R$ 1,951.9 million, equaling annual growth of 19% and surpassing our
guidancefortheyear.
Learnmoreaboutoperationalandfinancialdataonourinvestorrelationswebsite:https://ri.srna.co/en/.
4.2DescriçãodeindicadoresdeemissãodeGEEparaasatividadesdaorganização.Porexemplo,tCO2e/produtos
fabricados.
To determine the intensity of the company’s greenhouse gas emissions, we considered the sum of Scopes 1, 2,
and3,followingGRI305-4,relativetoallenergyproducedbySerenaEnergiaatitsoperationalunitslocatedin
Brazil and the United States. The final result was 0.54 tCO2e/GWh. Nonetheless, the company's Decarbonization
Plan focuses on the emissions intensity of Scope 1 + 2 in Brazil as the measurement’s unit for its reduction target
to ensure that our emissions can be monitored in line with the company's growth projections for the coming
years.
4.3DescriçãodeestratégiaseprojetosparaagestãodeemissõesdeGEE.
In terms of GHG emissions management, a major milestone was the replacement of more than 50% of fossil fuel
withbiofuelinoperations,contributingtothereductionofscope1emissions.
Improved management of fugitive gas emissions. This includes the implementation of preventive maintenance
practicespractices,morerigorousmonitoringandthedevelopmentofactionplanstomitigateleaks.
Improved control over scope 3 emissions related to travel, with a focus on improving data collection and
identifyingopportunitiesforreduction.
4.4 Informações sobre contratos com clientes e fornecedores que incluam cláusulas vinculadas à elaboração de
inventáriosdeGEEe/ouaoenviodeinformaçõesrelacionadas.
In 2023, our suppliers' emissions from implementation and operation activities were reported in the “Purchased
GoodsandServices”category.AspartoftheactionsoutlinedintheDecarbonizationPlan,aspecificclauseforthe
submission of information related to the GHG inventory was drawn up and approved in 2023. This clause was be
includedincontractssignedstartingJanuary2024.
4.5 Informações sobre incertezas, exclusões de fontes de dados e outras características da elaboração do

inventário.
As described in the Report, the quantification of greenhouse gas emissions is subject to inherent uncertainty due
toincompletescientificknowledgeusedtodeterminegasemissionfactorsandvaluesforcombiningdifferentgas
emissions. The quantification of greenhouse gases is subject to inherent uncertainty as a result of both scientific
uncertaintyandestimationuncertainty.Uncertaintiesmayarisebecauseof:
• inherentuncertaintyinthequantificationofactivitydataandemissionfactors,whichareusedinmathematical

SECTION A. Executive summary
KBS Certification Services Pvt. Ltd. has been commissioned by Porto do Delta Energia S.A. to perform an
independent verification of its registered CDM project, “Porto do Delta Wind Power Plant CDM Project”,
UNFCCC ref. no. 7027 for the reported GHG emission reductions for the given monitoring period 04/11/2016
to 31/12/2020 (both dates included). The CDM must undergo independent third-party verification and
certification of emission reductions as the basis for issuance of Certified Emission Reductions (CERs).
The CDM projects must undergo independent third-party verification and certification of emission reductions
as the basis for issuance of Certified Emission Reductions (CERs).
The objectives of this verification exercise are, by review of objective, to establish that:
 The project activity has been implemented and operated in accordance with the registered PDD or
any approved revised PDD;
 The actual monitoring systems and procedures are in compliance with the monitoring systems and
procedures described in the monitoring plan;
 The GHG emission reduction data and calculation have been assessed to correctly support the
emission reductions being claimed and that the reported GHG emission reduction data is free from
material misstatement;
 To verify that reported GHG emission data is sufficient supported by evidence.
Scope:
The scope of the verification is the independent and objective review and ex post determination of the
monitored reductions in GHG emission by the project activity. The verification is based on review of monitoring
report, supporting information and
(a) The registered PDD, including the monitoring plan and the corresponding validation opinion(s);
(b) Previous verification reports, deviation requests, Post registration changes;
(c) Monitoring report for the monitoring period under verification including CER calculations sheets and all
supporting documents;
(d) The applied monitoring methodology;
(e) The applied standardized baseline (if applicable);
(f) Relevant decisions, clarifications and guidance from the CMP and the CDM Executive Board;
(g) All information and references relevant to the project activity’s resulting in emission reductions
(h) The project is assessed against the requirements of the Kyoto Protocol, the CDM Modalities and
Procedures and related rules and guidance.
KBS has, based on the recommendations in the latest version of CDM Validation and Verification Standard for
project activity, employed a rule-based approach in the verification, focusing on the identification of significant
reporting risks and the reliability of project monitoring.
Description of project:
The “Porto do Delta Wind Power Plant CDM Project” consists in the construction and operation of a wind power
plant, located at Parnaíba city, Piauí state, Brazil with a generation capacity of 30.8 MW.
The plant supply clean electricity to the Brazilian National Interconnected System (SIN). The project displaces
power generation using fossil fuels and hence leads to a reduction in greenhouse gas emissions.
The project activity was implemented and the commercial operation date for the wind power plants have been
verified from the ANEEL Dispatches /21/.
Methodology:
KBS follows a rule-based verification approach, wherein, as a first step, the contract review is undertaken as
per latest version of CDM Accreditation Standard. Subsequently, after the contract is signed, the monitoring
report of the project activity is made publicly available at UNFCCC website as per CDM procedures. A desk
review of the project documentation is undertaken, which is followed by a remote site visit by the members of
verification team in accordance with the latest version of CDM AS. The verification protocol is filled by the
verification team that is based on standard auditing practices and version 03.0 of CDM VVS for project activity,
to capture the assessment of applicable CDM requirements viz., version 03.0 of CDM Project Standard for
project activity, registered PDD, applied methodology, applied standardized baseline and/or tools and recent
Version 04.0 Page 2 of 28
CDM-VCR-FORM
decisions. The verification protocol provides transparent means to record the observations and compliances
by the verification team members and the nonconformities, if any. The verification protocol is an internal
document and is available on request. Following are the major milestones for the verification under
consideration.
Publication of MR 12/11/2021
Remote site verification 10/12/2021

Final Verification Report 04/04/2022

SECTION D. Means of verification
D.1. Desk/document review
A desk review is undertaken, involving but not limited to,
 A review of the data and information presented to verify their completeness;
 A review of the registered PDD;
 A review of the monitoring plan and monitoring methodology, paying particular attention to the
frequency of measurements, the quality of metering equipment including calibration requirements, and
the quality assurance and quality control procedures;
 An evaluation of data management and the quality assurance and quality control system in the context
of their influence on the generation and reporting of emission reductions.

As result of the COVID-19 pandemic, taking into account the rules of relevant national and local authorities
(local to the DOE offices as well as to locality of the site visits), World Health Organization (WHO)
recommendations, policies of the DOE and other relevant travel restrictions and guidance (for example, a
requirement to self-isolate upon return from specific countries), a DOE may postpone site visits for onsite
inspections required by the “Validation and Verification Standard for project activities” version 03.0 /8/.
For this project, PP has made commitment/timeline /24/. Hence, the DOE has skipped the on-site visit /25/.
However, as per the CDM EB, the DOE may use other standard auditing techniques for validation or verification
as referred to in sections 9.1.3 of the VVS-PA /8/.
Verification team has used the following alternative means for its assessment and to justify that they are
sufficient for the purpose of verification. Along with desk review, audit team has conducted remote audit

SECTION F. Internal quality control
The draft verification report prepared by team leader is reviewed by an independent technical reviewer (having
competence of relevant technical area himself/herself or through an independent technical area expert) to
confirm the internal procedures established by KBS are duly followed and the verification report/opinion is
reached in an objective manner and complies with the applicable CDM requirements.
The independent technical reviewer may approve or reject the draft verification report. The findings may be
identified even at this stage, which needs to be satisfactorily resolved, before the request for issuance is
submitted to UNFCCC. The final decision is taken by the Manager Technical and Certification. The technical
reviewer and Manager (Technical & Certification) can be same person.
The final decision is authorized by Managing Director, KBS once the report is approved by the Manager
(Technical & Certification).
SECTION G. Verification opinion
The verification team confirms that the evidence is of sufficient quantity, appropriate quality and reliable. The
reported values, notation, units and sources in the monitoring report for all the monitoring parameters have
been cross checked with the emission reduction sheet and monitoring report. During the course of verification
and remote site visit, the data submitted by PP was cross verified with the values mentioned in the emission
reduction sheet and monitoring report. The procedure for data monitoring, recording, transfer and compilation
was also verified and found in compliance with the monitoring plan as mentioned in the registered PDD.
It is confirmed by the assessment team that the reported emission reductions have been conservatively
calculated. A list of referred documents for verification is also included in Appendix 3 of this report.
Based on the information seen and evaluated we confirm that the implementation of the project activity has
resulted in 254,737 tCO e emission reductions during monitoring period 04/11/2016 to 31/12/2020 (first and
2
last days are included).

SECTION H. Certification statement
KBS Certification Services Pvt. Ltd. has been contracted by Porto do Delta Energia S.A. to undertake
independent verification and certification for the greenhouse gas (GHG) emission reductions reported from the
“Porto do Delta Wind Power Plant CDM Project” and UNFCCC Reference Number 7027 for the monitoring
period 04/11/2016 to 31/12/2020 (including both dates) in the Monitoring Report Version 1 (first version) dated
10/11/2021.
The verification is based on the approved PDD and the monitoring report for this project. Our verification
approach was based on the requirements as defined under the Kyoto Protocol, as well as those defined by
the CDM Executive Board.
The management of the Porto do Delta Energia S.A. is responsible for the preparation of the GHG emissions
data and the reported GHG emissions reductions on the basis set out within the project Final Monitoring
Report, version 2.1 dated 26/01/2022. The calculation and determination of GHG emission reductions from
the project is the responsibility of the management of the Porto do Delta Energia S.A. The development and
maintenance of records and reporting procedures are in accordance with the Monitoring Report Version 2.1
dated 26/01/2022.
It is our responsibility to express an independent GHG verification opinion on the GHG emissions and on the
calculation of GHG emission reductions from the project for the period 04/11/2016 up to 31/12/2020 (including
both dates) based on the reported emission reductions in the Final Monitoring Report Version 2.1 dated
26/01/2022 for the same period.
Based on an understanding of the risks associated with reporting GHG emissions data and the controls in
place to mitigate these, KBS planned and performed our work to obtain the information and explanations that
we considered necessary to provide sufficient evidence for us to give reasonable assurance that this reported
amount of GHG emission reductions for the period is fairly stated.

SECTION A. Executive summary
KBS Certification Services Pvt. Ltd. has been commissioned by Omega Energia Renovavel S.A. to perform
an independent verification of its registered CDM project, “Delta do Parnaíba Wind Power Plant Complex CDM
Project”, UNFCCC ref. no. 8021 for the reported GHG emission reductions for the given monitoring period
01/03/2013 to 29/02/2020 (both dates included). The CDM must undergo independent third-party verification
and certification of emission reductions as the basis for issuance of Certified Emission Reductions (CERs).
The CDM projects must undergo independent third-party verification and certification of emission reductions
as the basis for issuance of Certified Emission Reductions (CERs).
The objectives of this verification exercise are, by review of objective, to establish that:
 The project activity has been implemented and operated in accordance with the registered PDD or
any approved revised PDD;
 The actual monitoring systems and procedures are in compliance with the monitoring systems and
procedures described in the monitoring plan;
 The GHG emission reduction data and calculation have been assessed to correctly support the
emission reductions being claimed and that the reported GHG emission reduction data is free from
material misstatement;
 To verify that reported GHG emission data is sufficient supported by evidence.
Scope:
The scope of the verification is the independent and objective review and ex post determination of the
monitored reductions in GHG emission by the project activity. The verification is based on review of monitoring
report, supporting information and
(a) The registered PDD or any approved revised PDD, including the monitoring plan and the corresponding
validation opinion(s);
(b) Previous verification reports, deviation requests, Post registration changes;
(c) Monitoring report for the monitoring period under verification including CER calculations sheets and all
supporting documents;
(d) The applied monitoring methodology;
(e) The applied standardized baseline (if applicable);
(f) Relevant decisions, clarifications and guidance from the CMP and the CDM Executive Board;
(g) All information and references relevant to the project activity’s resulting in emission reductions
(h) The project is assessed against the requirements of the Kyoto Protocol, the CDM Modalities and
Procedures and related rules and guidance.
KBS has, based on the recommendations in the latest version of CDM Validation and Verification Standard for
project activity, employed a rule-based approach in the verification, focusing on the identification of significant
reporting risks and the reliability of project monitoring.
Description of project:
The “Delta do Parnaíba Wind Power Plant Complex CDM Project” is a complex project composed of three
wind power plants (WPPs), Delta do Parnaíba, Porto das Barcas and Porto Salgado, located at Parnaíba city,
Piauí state, Brazil with a total generation capacity of 70 MW.
All plants supply clean electricity to the Brazilian National Interconnected System (SIN). The project displaces
power generation using fossil fuels and hence leads to a reduction in greenhouse gas emissions.
The project activity was implemented and the commercial operation date for the wind power plants have been
verified from the ANEEL Dispatches /20/.
Methodology:
KBS follows a rule-based verification approach, wherein, as a first step, the contract review is undertaken as
per latest version of CDM Accreditation Standard. Subsequently, after the contract is signed, the monitoring
report of the project activity is made publicly available at UNFCCC website as per CDM procedures. A desk
review of the project documentation is undertaken, which is followed by a remote site visit by the members of
verification team in accordance with the latest version of CDM AS. The verification protocol is filled by the
verification team that is based on standard auditing practices and version 03.0 of CDM VVS for project activity,
to capture the assessment of applicable CDM requirements viz., version 03.0 of CDM Project Standard for
Version 04.0 Page 3 of 38

SECTION D. Means of verification
D.1. Desk/document review
A desk review is undertaken, involving but not limited to,
 A review of the data and information presented to verify their completeness;
 A review of the approved revised PDD;
 A review of the monitoring plan and monitoring methodology, paying particular attention to the
frequency of measurements, the quality of metering equipment including calibration requirements, and
the quality assurance and quality control procedures;
 An evaluation of data management and the quality assurance and quality control system in the context
of their influence on the generation and reporting of emission reductions.
The list of documents reviewed is included in the section ‘Appendix 3’ of this report.

inspections required by the “Validation and Verification Standard for project activities” version 03.0 /8/.
For this project, PP has made commitment/timeline /25/. Hence, the DOE has skipped the on-site visit /26/.

as referred to in sections 9.1.3 of the VVS-PA /8/.

E.5. Compliance of the registered monitoring plan with applied methodologies, applied
standardized baselines, and other applied methodological regulatory documents
Means of verification The project applies the approved methodology ACM0002 – Consolidated
baseline methodology for grid-connected electricity generation from renewable
sources, version 12.3.0 /12/.
The approved revised PDD /5/ also refers to the following tools:
 TOOL01 Methodological tool: Tool for the demonstration and
assessment of additionality (Version 06.0.0) /13/;
 TOOL02 Methodological tool: Combined tool to identify the baseline
scenario and demonstrate additionality (Version 3.0.0) /14/;
 TOOL03 Methodological tool: Tool to calculate project or leakage CO2
emissions from fossil fuel combustion (Version 02.0) /15/;
 TOOL07 Methodological tool: Tool to calculate the emission factor for an
electricity system (Version 2.2.1) /16/.
The monitoring plan of the approved revised PDD /5/ was reviewed against the
monitoring requirements of the applied methodology and applicable tools.
Findings Refers to CAR 01 raised in section E.1 and successfully closed. Refer to
Appendix 4 for further details.
Conclusion As per para 357 and 358 of CDM VVS for project activity version 03.0 /8/, in the
opinion of the verification team the monitoring plan of the approved revised PDD
complies with the monitoring requirement of the applied approved methodology
ACM0002, version 12.3.0 /12/ in the context of the project activity.

E.7. Compliance with the calibration frequency requirements for measuring instruments
Means of verification The calibration certificates were checked in order to verify the compliance and the
calibration frequency of each measuring equipment.
The calibration frequency of the electricity meters is 5 years from 01/01/2017
onwards as per national standard /30/ and defined in the approved revised PDD
/5/. Therefore, as per national standard /30/ from the starting date of the first
crediting period until 31/12/2016, meters calibration should happen each 2 (two)
years and from 01/01/2017, each 5 (five years).
